evaluator.note: Benchmark v2. Blind subagent: packet-only context (prompt.md, diff-drift-report.md, raw-git-diff.patch); dispatched by the maintainer's agent session on 2026-06-10. Answer recorded verbatim.

findings.title: Hardcoded AWS access key committed to source
findings.evidence: Raw diff line: const AWS_ACCESS_KEY = "AKIAJW6PNQ4R7TLZ5KYM"; — an AKIA-prefixed AWS access key ID literal added to the JSX source, matching Diff Drift's high-severity flag.

findings.title: Credential shipped to and used in client-side code
findings.evidence: The key is passed to uploadDirect(event.target.files[0], AWS_ACCESS_KEY) inside a React component's onChange handler, so the credential would be bundled into the browser payload and exposed to every end user, not just stored in the repo.

notes: Diff Drift's report is corroborated by the raw diff: the flagged line appears verbatim and is the only flag, with no false positives observed.

The key must be treated as compromised once committed — remediation should include rotating/revoking the AWS credential, not just removing the line; browser uploads should use a presigned-URL or backend-mediated flow instead.
The accompanying changes (importing uploadDirect and wiring the onChange handler) are functionally reasonable for a direct-upload feature; the only trust issue is the embedded credential.
